$comment: Pre-set Azure deployment defaults so `deploy` runs in one shot. The azure-deployer agent MUST read this file and use these values WITHOUT asking the user. The only interactive step allowed is `az login` if no subscription is active; everything else is fixed here. Override a value only if the user explicitly asks.

subscription.$comment: AUTO = detect via `az account show --query id -o tsv`. If not logged in, run `az login` once, then re-detect. Never hard-code a subscription id.

naming.uniqueSuffixStrategy: lowercase 6-char hash of the subscription id; append to globally-unique names (web app, Key Vault, Postgres, Foundry, ACR) as <name>-<suffix>
naming.$comment: App/Key Vault/Postgres/Foundry names are globally unique, so append the deterministic suffix. RG and plan names need not be globally unique.

location.$comment: Hosting in koreacentral (low latency for the user). Azure AI Foundry / Azure OpenAI in eastus2 for broad model availability; cross-region calls to Foundry are fine. If gpt-4o-mini is unavailable in eastus2 at deploy time, fall back to swedencentral, then westus.

compute.$comment: Default App Service (Linux, Node 22). B1 supports Always On (needed so the Copilot CLI runtime doesn't cold-start-fail). If the in-process Copilot SDK spawn fails the ai-health probe, switch to containerapps with the CLI as a TCP sidecar + Azure Files volume for COPILOT_HOME.

database.$comment: Production Postgres. Admin password is generated at deploy time (32+ chars) and stored in Key Vault; CI sets DATABASE_URL from it. Local dev still uses SQLite. Allow Azure services + the App Service outbound IPs through the firewall.

foundry.$comment: The GitHub Copilot SDK BYOK backend, upgraded to GPT-5. Create TWO model deployments: the quality tier `gpt-5` (lectures, assessment — FOUNDRY_DEPLOYMENT_NAME) and the cost/speed tier `gpt-5-mini` (high-volume research extraction across parallel agents — FOUNDRY_FAST_DEPLOYMENT_NAME). modelVersion AUTO = latest available version in-region. The app wires each as provider:{type:'openai', baseUrl:'<endpoint>/openai/v1/', apiKey, wireApi:'responses'} + model = the deployment name. If gpt-5 is unavailable in-region, fall back in order: gpt-5.1 -> gpt-5-chat -> gpt-4.1 (and the fast tier: gpt-5-mini -> gpt-4.1-mini). Want the absolute top tier? swap `model` to gpt-5-pro or gpt-5.2 (higher cost/latency).

keyVault.name: kglearn-kv
keyVault.$comment: Holds the Foundry API key, Postgres admin password, AUTH_SECRET, and the search-provider key. App Settings reference Key Vault secrets. Name gets the unique suffix.

auth.$comment: AUTH_SECRET auto-generated at deploy. Default provider Entra ID: the agent creates an Entra app registration via `az ad app create`, sets the redirect URI to https://<webapp-host>/api/auth/callback/microsoft-entra-id, and stores client id/secret in Key Vault. If the user lacks Entra app-registration permission, fall back to a GitHub OAuth provider and surface that one app-registration step.

search.$comment: Research runs purely on the Copilot SDK (Azure Foundry gpt-5-mini) — it expands the prerequisite graph from the model's own knowledge, so NO web-search key is required to deploy or operate. Web search is an OPTIONAL enrichment: set provider:'tavily' + supply TAVILY_API_KEY (exempt from the Azure-only rule) only if you want live web-source citations. Absent → research still works fully.

cicd.$comment: GitHub Actions deploys on push to main via OIDC (federated credential created by the agent). CI flips Prisma to postgresql, runs `prisma migrate deploy`, runs the keystone test, deploys, then runs the post-deploy ai-health gate. A failing gate blocks release.

manualStepsAllowed: az login (one interactive browser auth if no active subscription)
supply TAVILY_API_KEY (OPTIONAL — only for live web citations; research works without it)
$finalComment: Everything not in manualStepsAllowed is pre-decided here. The deploy agent provisions, wires secrets, sets up CI, and reports the public URL without further questions.
